operating_ranges.evidence: Bearing Test Specifications: Bearing: 7006C Speed: 34000 min⁻¹
Preload: 4500N for Ø 30x Ø 62x Ø 15mm bearing
Temperature rise at different speeds: 1000rpm ~5°C to 3000rpm ~35°C depending on seal configuration
Suggested axial compression: 10-30μm for outer cap male register surface
Grease quantities range from 0.03 cc/brg for smallest bearings to 80 cc/brg for largest bearings

degradation_mechanisms.rate_description: 90% reduced grease leakage with sealed bearings
degradation_mechanisms.evidence: 90% Reduced Grease Leakage with sealed bearings vs open bearings
Water Contamination Ratio in Grease and need to prevent water and dust ingress

degradation_mechanisms.rate_description: 200 hours for brass cage vs 300 hours for PPS material under severe conditions
degradation_mechanisms.evidence: wear could be found in the Brass cage bearings after 200 hours compared to 300 hours for the PPS material
electrical resistance prevents pitting of the ball surface due to electrical discharge in built-in motor spindles

fault_conditions.name: excessive_temperature
fault_conditions.description: High temperature generation due to inadequate sealing or excessive preload
fault_conditions.symptoms: Temperature rise above normal operating range
fault_conditions.evidence: low friction design prevents high temperature generation and temperature rise comparison data

fault_conditions.name: cage_noise
fault_conditions.description: Friction between ball surface and cage guide surface in cold conditions
fault_conditions.symptoms: Audible cage noise particularly in cold conditions
fault_conditions.evidence: cage noise can occur in grease lubricated bearings due to friction between the ball surface and the cage guide surface; this can be particularly noticed in cold conditions

fault_conditions.name: grease_leakage
fault_conditions.description: Loss of lubrication due to grease escaping from bearing
fault_conditions.symptoms: 25% leakage in open bearings vs ~2% with seals

fault_conditions.name: contamination_ingress
fault_conditions.description: Water, dust, or coolant entering bearing causing degradation
fault_conditions.evidence: sealed bearings provide extra reliability in dusty and water/oil contaminated environments

maintenance_actions.name: grease_replenishment
maintenance_actions.evidence: The greasing process is very important to the successful performance of the bearing and grease quantities specified for different bearing sizes

maintenance_actions.name: running_in_procedure
maintenance_actions.what_it_resets: bearing operating temperature and grease distribution
maintenance_actions.evidence: Do not operate bearings at full spindle speed when bearings are first installed. It is necessary to run the grease in

maintenance_actions.name: preload_adjustment
maintenance_actions.evidence: bearing mounted preload can be reduced or increased by offsetting spacers

assumptions.statement: Sealed bearings use contacting seals with waterproof grease
assumptions.reason: Manual specifies sealed bearings have contacting type seals with special WPH waterproof grease

assumptions.statement: Hybrid bearings use ceramic balls with steel rings
assumptions.reason: Manual defines hybrid as steel ringed bearings using silicon nitride ceramic balls

assumptions.statement: Grease quantity is based on percentage of internal free space
assumptions.reason: Manual specifies 15% of internal free space for angular contact ball bearings
